member.office_address: 2446 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-4207

form_analysis.form_header_text: Congressman Matt Van Epps (TN-07): Appropriations Application for FY2027
form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Thank you for your interest in the Fiscal Year 2027 Congressional appropriations process. If you are interested in submitting information pertaining to programmatic or report language requests, please fill out this form as completely as possible for each request that you want considered. This is NOT the appropriate form for Community Project Funding (CPF) requests. To adhere to Appropriations Committee deadlines, electronic copies of this form should be returned no later than the dates listed below. This form will be updated with those dates when those deadlines are announced. There is no guarantee that your request will be supported. Early submissions are welcome and appreciated. For any further questions please contact Eric.Kim@mail.house.gov

sections.description: Details about the organization and individual submitting the request.

fields.label: Requesting Organization
fields.help_text: Name of the entity requesting the appropriation or language.

fields.label: Contact person(s) for this request?
fields.help_text: Name of the primary point of contact.

sections.description: Jurisdictional and agency alignment for the request.

fields.label: Appropriations Subcommittee
fields.help_text: Select the subcommittee with jurisdiction over this request.
options: National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs (NSRP//formerly SFOP)
fields.rationale: NASA falls under the jurisdiction of the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: Applicable Federal Department/Agency/Office (e.g. DHS/FEMA)
fields.help_text: Provide the specific agency or office associated with the request.
fields.rationale: The target agency for this request is NASA.

fields.label: Specific Account, Program, Activity, etc. (e.g. Assistance to Firefighter Grants)
fields.help_text: Identify the exact program or account within the agency.
fields.rationale: This is the specific account within NASA for which funding is being requested.

fields.label: Is this request being spearheaded with a letter from another Congressional office, and are you requesting that we sign onto that letter?  YES or NO?  (If YES, please include sponsor's office)
fields.help_text: Information on existing 'Dear Colleague' letters and sponsorship.
fields.draft_value: YES; Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA).
fields.rationale: The reference values indicate a house letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu exists.

sections.description: Specifics regarding funding amounts and proposed text.

fields.label: Description of Program Request or Proposed Bill/Report Language. If requesting language, please provide a complete draft of your requested language and whether it is bill or report language. If the draft does not fit below, please email this draft to Eric.Kim@mail.house.gov with "VanEpps FY27 Requested Report/Bill Language" in the subject line.
fields.help_text: Complete draft of requested language and description of the program.
fields.draft_value:
* $9,000,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) to fund Earth Science, Planetary Science, Astrophysics, Heliophysics, and Biological and Physical Sciences programs.
* Programmatic request for existing accounts to support peer-reviewed missions and competitive research grants defined by the National Academies' Decadal Surveys.
* Provides the necessary resources to restore stability to the space science portfolio following the budgetary disruptions of FY 2026.
fields.rationale: Directly reflects the $9 billion programmatic ask for the Science Mission Directorate as specified in the funding request context.

fields.label: Requested Funding Amount for FY27 (Note: These are the ONLY options offered by the Appropriations Committee)
fields.help_text: The nature of the funding request for the upcoming fiscal year.
options: Total funding level of $_____ (indicated funding level in next question)
Support the FY27 President's Budget Request
fields.draft_value: Total funding level of $_____ (indicated funding level in next question)
fields.rationale: The planetary society is asking for a specific programmatic topline of $9 billion.

fields.label: If requesting a specific funding amount, please indiciate the funding amoun (e.g. $100 million)
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Funding history for FY26 and President's requested funding for FY27 (if applicable)
fields.help_text: Previous fiscal year funding level and current budget request amount.
fields.draft_value: FY26 Enacted: $7,250,000,000; FY27 President's Budget Request: Currently unknown.
fields.rationale: Using the provided enacted figure for FY26 and noting the FY27 request is pending.

sections.description: Information supporting the request's relevance and background.

fields.label: Tennessee connection (as relevant)
fields.help_text: Describe how this request impacts or relates to Tennessee and the 7th District.
fields.draft_value: NASA Science has invested over $1.7M in TN-7 since FY23, including a $1.74M award to Vanderbilt University for ice-cloud radiometry and a grant to Austin Peay State University for planetary defense research.
fields.rationale: Cites specific local spending data for Vanderbilt and Austin Peay State University located in the district.

fields.label: Background information or other comments
fields.help_text: Any additional context or details for the request.
fields.draft_value:
* Direct local impact in TN-07 through $1,742,217 for Vanderbilt University (ice-cloud radiometer science) and grants to Austin Peay State University supporting Moon and Mars exploration.
* Maintains U.S. leadership against China's rapid expansion in space science, ensuring the United States continues to set the norms for 21st-century exploration.
* Protects the specialized aerospace workforce and STEM pipeline, building on the $1.7M+ in NASA Science obligations recently awarded to district institutions.
* Every $1 invested in NASA returns roughly $3 to the economy, driving innovation in weather forecasting, planetary defense, and advanced manufacturing.
fields.rationale: Cites specific TN-07 spending data for Vanderbilt and Austin Peay State University while incorporating strategic talking points regarding China and economic ROI.

top_contracts.description: Principal investigator and science management team support responsible for the management of the polarized submillimeter ice-cloud radiometer instrument mission for the NASA life cycle formulation and implementation phases a though f.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Science management supporting a submillimeter ice-cloud radiometer mission.

top_grants.description: Execute a balanced science program based on discipline-specific guidance from the national academies of sciences  engineering  and medicine  administration priorities  and direction from congress.   Participate as a key partner and enabler in the agency S exploration initiative  focusing on scientific research of  on  and from the moon  lunar orbit  Mars  and beyond.   Advance discovery in emerging fields by identifying and exploiting cross-disciplinary opportunities between traditional science disciplines   develop a directorate-wide  target-user focused approach to applied programs  including Earth science applications  space weather  planetary defense  and space situational awareness
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Guiding strategic science for Moon, Mars, and planetary defense.
